summary: Scorching China>Rider, soaring Row Jimmy, tight jams throughout
blurb: The set bursts with a monster China>Rider and a soaring Row Jimmy, while the second half rides high on Standing On The Moon and a powerful Dark Star. Brent’s organ work shines and both drummers lock in for an energetic groove. Overall the show delivers tight jams and standout vocal moments.
review: Alpine Valley on July 17‑1989 stands out as one of the band’s late‑summer high points. From the opening “Let The Good Times Roll” through a rock‑heavy first set, the night builds to a blistering second set where a monster China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider launches an extended groove that carries through Playing In The Band, Uncle John’s Band and into a soaring Standing On The Moon before the drums/space duo erupts into a transcendent Dark Star. Highlights include Jerry’s scorching leads on China>Rider and Row Jimmy, Brent’s lush organ fills and soaring backing vocals on Uncle John’s Band, and a heartfelt vocal moment during “And We Bid You Good Night”. The dual drumming of Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann drives the high‑octane energy, while Phil provides a solid bass foundation throughout. With crystal‑clear soundboard capture, this performance is both a sonic treat and a memorable chapter in the Dead’s 1989 tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across ten of eleven recordings (all but one mixed). Reviewers repeatedly praise the scorching solos, high‑energy second set, standout songs like China>Rider, Row Jimmy, Standing On The Moon and Dark Star, and consistently note excellent band cohesion. No negative performance commentary appears, just a single mixed note about missing details, warranting a robust +1.6 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent crystal‑clear soundboard mix that captures the full set with balanced vocals, instruments and no noticeable drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Monster China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider sequence ignites a high‑octane second set
Soaring Row Jimmy solo showcases Jerry’s fiery lead work
Standing On The Moon transitions into a transcendent Dark Star improvisation
Tight dual drumming and Brent’s lush organ fills drive the show’s energy
song_highlights: And We Bid You Good Night

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers scorching leads on China>Rider and Row Jimmy, plus a heartfelt vocal moment during the ‘Bid You Good Night’ finale.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid, tight bass foundation throughout; while one reviewer noted slightly weaker presence, most praise his consistent groove.
band_performance.Bob: Front‑center vocals are crisp and energetic, especially on The Music Never Stopped; minor flub noted but overall strong singing.
band_performance.Brent: Rocks the organ with lush fills, shines on Uncle John's Band backing vocals, and adds visible excitement during the huddle and jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Locks in tight, energetic drumming that fuels the second‑set fire and drives transitions into Space.
band_performance.Billy: Drums are powerful and responsive, complementing Mickey to create a driving rhythmic backbone.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
And We Bid You Good Night
